methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $60M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Tenna Systems is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Avner and Gabriel Bendheim, currently valued at $60M post-money after raising $13.5M. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 10 private frontier-tech firms in Defense & Security alongside 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS), with top private peers including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier to the $60M last post-money valuation, implying $150M. Peer-median analysis references four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and NODA AI ($125M Series A)—yielding a median valuation of $225M and a range of $125M–$500M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a low of $128M, median of $188M, and high of $259M. This range reflects reasonable upside from the current $60M valuation while anchoring to peer trading multiples in the defense technology space. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to comparables, the competitive positioning of Tenna's specific offering within the crowded defense-tech landscape, and near-term funding trajectory. The wide comp range ($125M–$500M) also indicates material variance in how the market prices Defense & Security companies at the Series A stage.
